title: China will buy more U.S. oil because it is a natural trade partner, says Energy Secretary Wright
summary_full: China relies heavily on crude oil imports from the Middle East but those supplies are mostly  cut off due to Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.
summary_public: China relies heavily on crude oil imports from the Middle East but those supplies are mostly  cut off due to Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

IR.label: negative
IR.evidence: Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz
